title: Coding Challenge: TypeScript Strict Mode Fundamentals

challenges.prompt: Write a function `countStrictFlags(config)` that takes a tsconfig.json compilerOptions object and returns the number of strict mode sub-flags that are explicitly set to true. Count only these nine flags: noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks, strictFunctionTypes, strictBindCallApply, strictPropertyInitialization, noImplicitThis, alwaysStrict, useUnknownInCatchVariables, exactOptionalPropertyTypes.

test_cases.name: All nine flags
test_cases.input: { noImplicitAny: true, strictNullChecks: true, strictFunctionTypes: true, strictBindCallApply: true, strictPropertyInitialization: true, noImplicitThis: true, alwaysStrict: true, useUnknownInCatchVariables: true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true }

test_cases.name: Mixed true and false
test_cases.input: { noImplicitAny: true, strictNullChecks: false, strictFunctionTypes: true }

challenges.prompt: Write a function `hasCriticalFlags(config)` that returns true if a tsconfig compilerOptions object has BOTH noImplicitAny and strictNullChecks set to true (the two critical flags for Meridian), false otherwise.
Write a function `isViteReady(config)` that returns true if a tsconfig has both strict:true AND isolatedModules:true (required for Vite), false otherwise. Check the exact property names.
